preheader: This weekend in Celebration: live music on the lake, open houses, sweet local deals, and good news — from Sunni. ☀️
forecast:
Well, good mornin', Celebration! Pour yourself some sweet tea and pull up a chair — it's a beauty out there. 89° and blue skies clear through Sunday, with a quick afternoon sprinkle around 4 to keep the lawns happy. Pool mornings, porch evenings — that's the assignment, hunni.

The 4th is one week out and the whole town's hummin'. I rounded up your weekend, three reads worth your while, the open houses you'll want to walk, and a deal that'll make you grin. — Sunni

top_billing.title: Summer Nights: Live Music on the Lakefront
top_billing.when: Saturday · 7:00 PM · The Lakeside Lawn · Free
top_billing.body: If you do just one thing this weekend, sugar, make it this one. The summer concert series is back out on the water — bring a blanket, grab a bite from the food trucks, and stay for that sunset over the lake. Lawns open at 6, music at 7. I'll save you a spot.

events.name: Food Truck Friday
events.meta: Town Center · 5–9 PM · 12+ trucks, live DJ

events.name: Lake Nona Night Market
events.meta: Boxi Park · 4–9 PM · worth the short drive

blog.title: 12 Ways to Beat the Florida Heat Without Leaving Celebration
blog.tease: Splash pads, shady patios, and the AC-blasted spots locals swear by.

blog.title: Hurricane Season Prep: The Celebration Checklist
blog.tease: What to stock, what to ignore, and the one thing everyone forgets.

blog.title: The Story Behind Market Street's Newest Café
blog.tease: Two neighbors, one espresso machine, and a very good croissant.

storefront.title: Meet the folks at Bloom & Vine
storefront.body: You've walked past the little flower shop on Front Street a hundred times — but did you know owner Maria started it from a card table at the farmers market three years ago? We sat down with her to talk slow mornings, local growers, and why she'll always hand-tie a bouquet for a stranger having a hard day.

bright_spot.title: The Pattersons turned a lemonade stand into $5,000 for neighbors in need
bright_spot.body: What started as three kids and a folding table on Sycamore Street has become a summer tradition — and this year, every cup sold went straight to the Osceola food bank. We met the family behind Celebration's sweetest little fundraiser.

guide.label: The Almanac
guide.title: Your 4th of July in Celebration
guide.body: The complete, no-stress guide to Independence Day around town — where to catch the fireworks, the parade route, family-friendly cookouts, and the spots that book up fast. Save it, share it, plan around it.

deals.name: The Corner Scoop
deals.desc: Buy one, get one free — all weekend

alist.name: Lakeside Dental
alist.desc: Family & cosmetic care. New patients welcome.

alist.name: Front Street Bistro
alist.desc: Wood-fired Italian with the best patio in town.

giveaway.title: Win a $100 Market Street gift card
giveaway.body: Spend it however you like — dinner, a fresh bouquet, a new pair of shades. Enter in ten seconds; I'll draw one lucky neighbor at the end of the month. Pop back each Thursday to boost your odds — it's on us, hunni.

did_you_know.headline: Celebration was dreamed up by Disney back in 1996.
did_you_know.body: And every December, Market Street still "snows" on the hour — soap-foam flurries and all. Now you've got something to say at the cookout. 😉
